skill.name: artifacts-builder
skill.description: Build complex Claude artifact projects with React, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui, then bundle them into a single HTML artifact.
skill.summary: Scaffold and bundle multi-component React artifacts for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: AI review found no evidence of malicious intent, prompt injection, credential access, or data exfiltration. Most high-severity static hits are false positives from Apache license text, CSS design tokens, and documentation examples. The remaining risk is medium because the skill runs shell scripts, installs npm packages, writes project files, removes build output, and extracts a bundled component archive.

medium_findings.title: External Dependency Installation
medium_findings.description: The scripts install pnpm, create a Vite project, and add npm packages. This is expected for a frontend scaffolding skill, but it executes package manager code from external registries and should be run only in trusted workspaces.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The package manager commands are direct and intentional. No malicious endpoint or credential exfiltration is present, but npm installs can run third-party lifecycle scripts.

medium_findings.title: Project File Generation and Configuration Writes
medium_findings.description: The initialization script writes configuration files and modifies TypeScript settings in the generated project. This is legitimate scaffolding behavior, but it can overwrite files if run in an unexpected location.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are visible and scoped to generated project files after changing into the requested project directory. The behavior is expected but still modifies local files.

medium_findings.title: Bundling Cleanup Removes Build Output
medium_findings.description: The bundling script removes dist and bundle.html before rebuilding. It checks for package.json and index.html first, but users should run it only from the intended project root.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The removal command is explicit and limited to predictable build outputs. The guard checks reduce accidental use, but wrong-directory execution can still delete local build artifacts.

medium_findings.title: Bundled Component Archive Extraction
medium_findings.description: The initialization script extracts a bundled shadcn component archive into the generated src directory. This supports offline component setup, but compressed archives should be reviewed when sourced from a community skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The tar extraction target is a generated project directory and there is no evidence of path traversal in the script itself. The archive contents are a supply-chain review concern for community distribution.

low_findings.title: Benign Documentation and License URLs
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL findings point to Apache license references, shadcn documentation, and a generated schema URL. They are references or configuration metadata, not active data exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are license text, user documentation, or generated component configuration. No code sends local data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are Apache license words and CSS color token names such as destructive. No hash, cipher, or cryptographic API is used at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirmed these are natural language or theme token matches. There is no crypto library call, digest construction, or password handling.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command Examples Are Expected Usage
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md command snippets tell users how to run the included scripts. They are not hidden execution paths and do not include suspicious shell syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands directly invoke the skill scripts described by the documentation. They are visible setup steps for the user, not concealed execution.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Hits Are Style and Testing Guidance
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance locations are design guidance and optional references to testing tools. No command gathers host, network, user, or environment information beyond basic Node version detection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text does not inspect the system. The only system check found is Node version detection, which is necessary for choosing a compatible Vite version.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Dependency Installation
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts install pnpm, create a Vite project, and add npm packages. This is expected for a frontend scaffolding skill, but it executes package manager code from external registries and should be run only in trusted workspaces.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The package manager commands are direct and intentional. No malicious endpoint or credential exfiltration is present, but npm installs can run third-party lifecycle scripts.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bundling Cleanup Removes Build Output
dangerous_patterns.description: The bundling script removes dist and bundle.html before rebuilding. It checks for package.json and index.html first, but users should run it only from the intended project root.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The removal command is explicit and limited to predictable build outputs. The guard checks reduce accidental use, but wrong-directory execution can still delete local build artifacts.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bundled Component Archive Extraction
dangerous_patterns.description: The initialization script extracts a bundled shadcn component archive into the generated src directory. This supports offline component setup, but compressed archives should be reviewed when sourced from a community skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The tar extraction target is a generated project directory and there is no evidence of path traversal in the script itself. The archive contents are a supply-chain review concern for community distribution.

content.value_statement: Complex Claude artifacts often need component structure, routing, state, and reliable bundling. This skill creates a modern React workspace and packages it into one shareable HTML artifact.
actual_capabilities: Initializes a React 18 and TypeScript artifact project with Vite.
Installs Tailwind CSS 3.4.1, Radix UI packages, and shadcn/ui dependencies.
Configures Tailwind themes, path aliases, PostCSS, and Vite aliases.
Extracts prepackaged shadcn/ui components into the generated source directory.
Bundles a React project into one HTML file using Parcel and html-inline.
limitations: Requires Node.js 18 or higher and package manager access.
Installs packages from npm, so dependency trust remains the user responsibility.
Best suited for complex React artifacts, not simple static snippets.
Does not automatically run browser tests or visual checks.

use_cases.title: Interactive Product Prototype
use_cases.description: Create a polished React artifact with shadcn/ui controls, stateful screens, and a bundled HTML handoff.

use_cases.title: Data Dashboard Artifact
use_cases.description: Build a multi-panel dashboard artifact with charts, filters, tabs, and reusable UI components.

use_cases.title: Reusable UI Demo
use_cases.description: Scaffold a Vite workspace for experimenting with Tailwind styles, Radix components, and artifact packaging.

prompt_templates.title: Start a New Artifact
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the artifacts-builder skill to initialize a new React artifact project named customer-dashboard. Keep the setup standard.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Component-Based Prototype
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a React artifact for a task planner with tabs, dialogs, filters, and saved local state. Use shadcn/ui components where they fit.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Shareable Bundle
prompt_templates.prompt: Bundle the current artifact project into a single HTML file and summarize the generated output and any setup commands used.

prompt_templates.title: Refine and Package a Complex UI
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve this artifact with responsive layouts, accessible controls, and clear state transitions, then package it as one HTML artifact.

output_examples.input: Create a project for an operations dashboard artifact.
output_examples.output: A React TypeScript workspace is created with Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui components, path aliases, and ready-to-edit source files.

output_examples.input: Bundle the completed artifact for sharing in Claude.
output_examples.output: A single HTML artifact is produced with application code, styles, and assets inlined for direct sharing.

output_examples.input: Add shadcn/ui controls to a prototype artifact.
output_examples.output: The artifact uses installed UI components such as tabs, dialogs, buttons, forms, and tooltips within the generated React project.

best_practices: Run the scripts inside a clean project workspace that can be safely modified.
Review npm dependency changes before publishing or sharing the final artifact.
Bundle only after the artifact works locally and required assets are included.
anti_patterns: Do not run the bundling script from an unrelated project root.
Do not use this skill for a simple one-file HTML artifact.
Do not treat generated dependencies as reviewed production code without inspection.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a React, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui project for complex Claude artifacts.

faq.question: Can it produce a single HTML artifact?
faq.answer: Yes. The bundling script uses Parcel and html-inline to create one bundle.html file.

faq.question: Does it require internet access?
faq.answer: Usually yes. The scripts install dependencies from npm unless the packages are already available locally.

faq.question: Is it safe for production code?
faq.answer: It is a scaffolding tool. Review dependencies, generated files, and bundled output before production use.

faq.question: When should I avoid using it?
faq.answer: Avoid it for simple static artifacts where direct HTML, CSS, or JSX would be faster.

faq.question: Does it test the artifact automatically?
faq.answer: No. Testing is optional and should be done with separate browser or Playwright workflows when needed.
